introQueue.label: Bit of joy
introQueue.name: What's brought you a bit of joy recently?
introQueue.description: 'A bit of' removes pressure to perform happiness. PERMA positive emotion — activates openness.

introQueue.label: Agenda check
introQueue.name: Before we get into it, anything you want to make sure we cover today?
introQueue.description: Semi-set early question. Gives the report explicit permission to set the agenda before the manager's plan takes over.

introQueue.label: Friction scan
introQueue.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
introQueue.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

introQueue.label: Opening pace check
introQueue.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
introQueue.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

focus_points.label: What they're saying yes and no to lately.
focus_points.reason: Whether the context switching is coming from shifting priorities, and what the top three outcomes are this cycle—worth clarifying what they’re intentionally deprioritizing.

focus_points.label: Context switching load and calendar control.
focus_points.reason: Whether the pace is sustainable given the context switching—could be too many interrupts, unclear ownership, or just a temporary surge that needs pacing.

focus_points.label: What they want more air cover on.
focus_points.reason: What he'd want more of from you when priorities keep moving—air cover, faster decisions, or helping unblock cross-team churn so he can stay in flow.

focus_points.label: How well updates are landing across stakeholders.
focus_points.reason: How they're communicating tradeoffs and status during churn—UX leads often get pulled into many loops, and crisp written updates prevent rework and misalignment.

brief.coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in is likely about how your UX Lead is handling priority churn without losing focus, sustainability, or stakeholder alignment.
brief.openingQuestion: What is pulling you across the most contexts right now, and what would you most want to protect as your top three outcomes this cycle?
listenFor: whether they name specific meetings, channels, or stakeholders that are driving interrupts
if they hesitate or keep revising what the top priorities are while talking
if they volunteer one concrete area where your faster decisions or air cover would reduce churn
avoid: do not jump straight into solving their calendar before you know which switches are necessary versus avoidable
do not turn this into a status review; keep the focus on tradeoffs, load, and lead-level ownership
brief.goodOutcome: You and Test have agreed which lead-level outcomes he will protect this cycle and one kind of cross-team churn you will help absorb so he can keep end-to-end UX ownership.
brief.suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where shifting priorities or stakeholder churn seemed to fragment his focus so you can test it without speaking in generalities.

issues: listenFor may lack observable behavioural cue: "if they hesitate or keep revising what the top priorities ar…"

queueRef.label: Bit of joy
queueRef.name: What's brought you a bit of joy recently?
queueRef.description: 'A bit of' removes pressure to perform happiness. PERMA positive emotion — activates openness.

queueRef.label: Agenda check
queueRef.name: Before we get into it, anything you want to make sure we cover today?
queueRef.description: Semi-set early question. Gives the report explicit permission to set the agenda before the manager's plan takes over.

queueRef.label: Friction scan
queueRef.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
queueRef.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

queueRef.label: Opening pace check
queueRef.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
queueRef.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

queueRef.label: Set the scene
queueRef.name: What's been most on your mind at work lately?
queueRef.description: Lets Test set context before anything more specific lands.

queueRef.label: Switching cost
queueRef.name: Where is the context switching hitting you hardest right now?
queueRef.description: Surfaces the specific part of the load that is hardest to absorb.

queueRef.label: Noise source
queueRef.name: What keeps pulling you out of flow most often?
queueRef.description: Finds the main interrupt pattern behind the churn.

queueRef.label: Priority tradeoff
queueRef.name: What are you choosing not to do this cycle?
queueRef.description: Forces the hidden trade-off behind current priorities.

queueRef.label: Decision support
queueRef.name: Where do you need faster calls from me?
queueRef.description: Surfaces where manager support would reduce churn or unblock progress.

queueRef.label: Update quality
queueRef.name: What part of your updates is hardest to keep crisp when things are moving?
queueRef.description: Checks how communication holds up under shifting priorities and stakeholder pressure.

queueRef.label: Stakeholder churn
queueRef.name: Which stakeholder loop is creating the most rework for you?
queueRef.description: Identifies where cross-functional churn is costing time and attention.

queueRef.label: Air cover
queueRef.name: What would make the next two weeks feel more protected?
queueRef.description: Finds the concrete support that would reduce friction without opening a new agenda.

queueRef.label: Next move
queueRef.name: What's the first thing you want to get under control before our next check-in?
queueRef.description: Closes by turning the conversation into one concrete next step.

closer.label: Next move
closer.name: What's the first thing you want to get under control before our next check-in?
closer.description: Closes by turning the conversation into one concrete next step.
